title: BD-007: Create shared Svelte layout components
description:
### BD-007: Create shared Svelte layout components
- **Type**: Foundation
- **Context**: Layouts per `09-UI-ROUTES-PHASE1.md` Section 2. Design system per `07-UI-UX-SPECS.md`. Component mapping per `07-UI-UX-SPECS.md` Section 6.
- **Dependencies**: BD-001.
- **Acceptance Criteria**:
  - `AppShell.svelte`: wraps all auth pages using DaisyUI `navbar` (header) + custom footer.
  - `AdminLayout.svelte`: extends AppShell using DaisyUI `drawer` + `menu` (240px sidebar nav).
  - `MobileLayout.svelte`: full-width using DaisyUI `navbar` (top) + `dock` (bottom bar).
  - `DisplayLayout.svelte`: minimal chrome for kiosk mode (DaisyUI `navbar` header only).
  - `StatusFooter.svelte`: connection indicator, queue count, clock.
  - `Toast.svelte` + `toastStore`: notification system using DaisyUI `toast` + `alert` components.
  - `OfflineBanner.svelte`: listens to `navigator.onLine`, shows/hides DaisyUI `alert alert-warning` banner.
  - `Modal.svelte`: generic `<dialog>` wrapper using DaisyUI `modal` + `modal-box`.
  - All layouts use DaisyUI semantic classes per `07-UI-UX-SPECS.md` — no raw utility-only styling for standard components.

title: Station/Display: real-time status update after send to next process
description:
After staff actions like 'Send to next process', the status (e.g. no longer 'Now Serving', queue list) should update in real time without manual refresh.

Current state:
- Backend already broadcasts StatusUpdate to station.{id} and NowServing to global.queue when session status changes (SessionService).
- Station page (Station/Index.svelte) listens to station.{id} only for StationNoteUpdated, not for StatusUpdate.
- Display Board listens to display.activity for activity feed; may need to refetch or listen for now_serving/queue changes.

Tasks:
- Station page: subscribe to StatusUpdate (and if relevant NowServing) on the station channel and refetch queue data (or update local state) so the queue list and 'Now Serving' state update immediately after transfer/complete/serve.
- Display board: ensure 'Now Serving' and waiting list refresh in real time when status changes (confirm display.activity or global.queue usage and refetch/update as needed).
- Optionally: ensure other UIs that show queue/status (e.g. triage) update in real time where applicable.

Files: resources/js/Pages/Station/Index.svelte, resources/js/Pages/Display/Board.svelte; backend events StatusUpdate, NowServing in app/Events.
close_reason: Station: listen .status_update on station.{id}, refetch queue; Display: subscribe global.queue .now_serving/.queue_length, refreshBoardData

Follow-up verification (run manually when ready). Check that the following work as intended. This list is extensible—add more items here as needed for future follow-ups.

**1. flexiqueue-i87 — Require permission OFF: override with reason only**
- Program with require_permission_before_override OFF: from Station, trigger override or force-complete. UI should show only reason (and target track for override); no PIN/QR/request step. Submit with valid reason → succeeds. Program with setting ON → PIN/QR/request still required.

**2. flexiqueue-5l7 — Process expected time + track total + estimated wait**
- Program Show Processes: set expected time (mm:ss) on a process; create/edit and list show it. Tracks tab: track payload has total_estimated_minutes (and travel_queue_minutes). After a client scans on /display, status page shows Estimated wait (~N minutes) when applicable.

**3. flexiqueue-rla — Triage and Track Overrides touch targets**
- On mobile viewport (~375px): Triage page (category buttons, track select, Look up, Confirm) and Track Overrides (Generate PIN/QR, Approve/Reject, modal controls) have comfortable tap targets (min 48px). No layout break; options don’t feel overly sparse on desktop.

Stack additional verification items in this bead description as you add more features to verify later.

Update data model (fields like expiry_mode, max_uses, used_count, expires_at), services, validation, and UI for admins/supervisors.

Note: The existing SessionController logic for 3 repeated PIN/QR/override permission checks is already implemented and considered done; this bead only covers the new expiry mode behavior, not redoing that permission logic.

title: Enqueue-back button from serving to queue
description:
Feature: When a staff member is currently calling/serving a token at a station, provide a button to 'enqueue' the token back into the queue for that station/program. This should move the person back to the appropriate queue position (e.g., end of line or per current routing rules) so they can start over in line for that station, instead of completing or cancelling the token. Update backend state transitions and station UI to support this action safely (logging, limits, and visibility).

Also refine the 'Mark no show' flow:
- If the staff clicks 'Mark no show', they should be able to decide whether to also enqueue the token back to the queue or not. If they choose NOT to enqueue, the token effectively stays at the front / next-to-be-called position as it behaves now.
- When a token is at its configured max no-show count (e.g. 2/3 going to 3/3), provide an extra action like 'Extend no show' so staff can allow additional calls (e.g. 4/3) without cancelling the token yet.
- Only when staff confirms this is the last call should they proceed with the existing 'Mark no show' + confirmation modal that cancels the token.
- The call/no-show counters (e.g. 1/3, 4/3, etc.) must continue to persist even if the token is enqueued back to the queue (current behavior).

title: BD-004: Create Eloquent models with relationships and business rule scopes
description:
### BD-004: Create Eloquent models with relationships and business rule scopes
- **Type**: Foundation
- **Context**: Relationships per `04-DATA-MODEL.md` Section "Relationship Summary". Business rules per Section "Key Business Rules".
- **Dependencies**: BD-003.
- **Acceptance Criteria**:
  - Models: `Program`, `ServiceTrack`, `TrackStep`, `Station`, `Token`, `Session`, `TransactionLog`, `User`.
  - All `belongsTo`, `hasMany`, `hasOne` relationships defined.
  - `TransactionLog` model: `update()` and `delete()` throw exceptions (append-only enforcement).
  - Scopes: `Session::scopeActive()` (status in waiting, serving), `Program::scopeActive()` (is_active = true).
  - `User` model: `role` cast as enum, `override_pin` hidden from serialization.
  - `Token` model: `qr_code_hash` in `$guarded` (immutable after creation).
  - Casts: `capabilities` as array (if needed later), `metadata` as array on `TransactionLog`.

title: BD-006: Create role-based authorization middleware and policies
description:
### BD-006: Create role-based authorization middleware and policies
- **Type**: Foundation
- **Context**: RBAC per `05-SECURITY-CONTROLS.md` Section 3. Middleware: `role:admin`, `role:admin,supervisor`, etc.
- **Dependencies**: BD-005.
- **Acceptance Criteria**:
  - `EnsureRole` middleware: checks `auth()->user()->role` against allowed roles. Returns 403 if unauthorized.
  - Registered in `bootstrap/app.php` as `role` alias.
  - Route groups applied: admin routes require `role:admin`, override routes require `role:admin,supervisor`.
  - `SessionPolicy`: validates station-scoped access (staff can only act on their assigned station).
  - `StationPolicy`: staff can only view their assigned station's queue.
  - Policy registered in `AuthServiceProvider`.
  - Tests: staff cannot access admin routes (403), admin can access everything.

title: BD-005: Implement session-based authentication (login + logout)
description:
### BD-005: Implement session-based authentication (login + logout)
- **Type**: Foundation
- **Context**: Auth per `05-SECURITY-CONTROLS.md` Sections 2.1–2.3. Laravel session driver = database.
- **Dependencies**: BD-003, BD-004.
- **Acceptance Criteria**:
  - Laravel Breeze or manual auth scaffolding (login form + controller).
  - `POST /login` authenticates via email + password.
  - On success: redirect based on role (admin → `/admin/dashboard`, staff → `/station`).
  - On failure: 422 with validation errors.
  - `POST /logout` invalidates session, redirects to `/login`.
  - Rate limiting: 5 attempts / 15 minutes (Laravel `ThrottlesLogins`).
  - Session driver set to `database` in `.env`.
  - `Login.svelte` page renders with Inertia.
  - Auth middleware applied to all non-public routes.

Document choice in architecture; implement logging in ProgramService.activate and ProgramService.deactivate.

title: [HIGH] Configurable token-to-client identity binding
description:
Context
- Today tokens are not bound to human-identifiable client records, which makes later person-level tracing and attendance analytics difficult.
- Triage already acts as a gate/attendance point; we want to reuse that to bind tokens to clients when appropriate.
- We must respect existing security/privacy policies: minimal PII, strong access control, encryption, and clear separation between token/session data and client identity data.

Goal
- Add a program-level configuration that lets admins decide whether token-to-client identity binding is required/optional/disabled for that program.
- When enabled, triage UX will support binding each token to a  using multiple ID methods, with strong safeguards against impersonation, especially on any public-facing triage surfaces.
- This bead is **design + end-to-end implementation of the binding feature** (backend + triage UI + security), but **NOT** any downstream attendance analytics/automation that use this data (future bead).

High-level behavior
- Per-program setting (e.g. ):
  - : current behavior (no client binding; tokens remain effectively anonymous).
  - : staff-facing triage can bind tokens to clients, but token creation still works without binding; public triage (if any) has stricter limits (see below).
  - : staff-facing triage must bind every token to a client before completing triage.
  - (If this is too much for Phase 1, minimum is a boolean  per program; confirm in spec before implementation.)
- New  concept/table: minimal PII for each person we may bind tokens to.
  - Fields (proposed – confirm against data model/security docs before finalizing):
    - uid=1000(sarmi) gid=1000(sarmi) groups=1000(sarmi),4(adm),24(cdrom),27(sudo),30(dip),46(plugdev),100(users),1001(docker) (PK)
    -  (string)
    -  (integer; *not* full DOB; aligns with privacy requirement to reduce granularity)
    - timestamps
  - Separate table(s)/relation(s) for ID documents and token bindings (see below) so that core session/token data stays mostly pseudonymous.
- Token-to-client binding: each token may be associated with exactly one  at creation/triage time (no retroactive rebind for Phase 1 unless explicitly required).

Identification methods / UX flows
1) Primary method – ID scan / ID entry
- At triage, if binding is enabled, the first-class path is to capture an ID presented by the client:
  - Staff (or kiosk scanner) can:
    - Scan a QR/barcode from an ID card; or
    - Manually type in the ID number and select the ID type.
- System behavior:
  - Look up an existing  via the ID (see security section for how lookup + encryption/hashing is done).
  - If match is found: show a minimal confirmation summary (e.g. name + birth year + partial ID type, but never full raw ID number) and let the staff confirm binding.
  - If no match is found: prompt to create a new .
    - Collect only: , , and the ID type/number being used.
    - After creation, bind current token to the new client.
- Multiple IDs per client:
  - A client can have multiple ID documents (e.g. national ID + health card).
  - When a new ID is scanned and the system does not find it, the staff can choose to:
    - Create a *new* client record; **or**
    - Search for an existing client by name/birth year and attach this ID to that client (see fallback flow).
  - This requires a  table (or equivalent) with roughly:
    - uid=1000(sarmi) gid=1000(sarmi) groups=1000(sarmi),4(adm),24(cdrom),27(sudo),30(dip),46(plugdev),100(users),1001(docker) (PK)
    -  (FK)
    -  (enum or reference table: national ID, driver's license, PhilHealth, etc.)
    -  (encrypted at rest; see below)
    - optional  for lookups without storing plaintext.

2) Supported ID types
- Support multiple ID types from day 1; exact list to be aligned with local context (national ID, driver's license, health card, etc.).
- Implementation details:
  - Use either an enum or a lookup table for ID types, following existing conventions.
  - UX should present a clear, human-friendly label for each type.
  - System MUST never expose full raw ID numbers back to the UI after initial entry, except in restricted admin views if explicitly allowed by security spec.

3) Fallback method – name + birth year (no physical ID)
- If the client does not have or does not present an ID, provide a fallback binding path:
  - Staff enters  and .
  - System performs a search over existing  and shows possible matches.
  - Staff must select which client to bind to, or explicitly choose to create a new client record.
- Collision handling / safeguards:
  - When multiple matches exist (same or similar name + same birth year):
    - Show a list with minimal identifying information and possibly a warning banner (e.g. 'Multiple clients match these details – confirm with the client before selecting.').
    - Log the selection for auditing (who performed the binding, from which station, timestamp).
  - Consider requiring staff-only access for this search flow (see access control).
- Data captured for fallback-created records is the same minimal set:  and  only (no ID numbers).

Security, privacy, and access control
- Encryption and storage of ID numbers:
  - Never store raw ID numbers in plaintext columns.
  - Use Laravel's encryption facilities for  so that values are encrypted at rest.
  - For fast lookup, consider storing a normalized hash of the ID number (e.g. SHA-256 of a canonicalized string) in .
    - Lookups happen via hash; the encrypted value is for recovery/verification only when allowed.
- Minimal PII principle:
  -  should store only: name + birth year + timestamps.
  - Anything more sensitive (full DOB, address, phone, etc.) is out of scope for this bead unless explicitly added to the spec.
- Separation of concerns:
  - Keep token/session information separate; the token-to-client association is via a join table/foreign key so it can be controlled and audited.
  - Ensure that existing reports that assume anonymous tokens are either updated or left unchanged when binding is disabled.
- Access control:
  - Name/birth-year search and manual ID entry flows should be **staff-only**.
  - Public/self-service triage should, by default, avoid free-form name search to reduce impersonation risk.
    - If public triage must support binding, constrain it to: scan-only flow, or extra verification, per security spec.
  - Enforce role/permission checks in controllers/services per  and security docs (e.g. only staff/admin roles can bind tokens or view client binding info).
- Audit logging:
  - For every binding operation, log:
    - , 
    - acting user, station, program, timestamp
    - method used (ID scan, name+birthyear, manual attach of new ID to existing client)
  - These logs are important for later investigations if a wrong identity is used (by mistake or intent).

Triage UX considerations
- Staff-facing triage (trusted environment):
  - Can show ID scan/entry, name+birth-year search, and explicit controls to create/bind client records.
  - If binding is required by program config, triage cannot complete without a binding; show clear error/inline validation.
- Public/self-service triage (if applicable):
  - Default: **no name search** to avoid easy impersonation.
  - If scanning an ID is supported in public mode, ensure the UI clearly explains what is happening ('We're linking your visit to your ID for X reasons'), and follow security docs for consent/notice.
- The bead should specify clearly which existing triage surfaces/pages are in scope (e.g. staff dashboard triage vs kiosk triage).

Non-goals / deferrals
- This bead **does NOT** include:
  - Attendance analytics or reports built on top of client binding.
  - Bulk migration of historical tokens to client records.
  - Complex identity resolution/deduplication algorithms beyond simple matching and staff-driven choice.
- These should be separate beads that depend on this one once binding is in place.

Acceptance criteria (high level)
- Admin can configure a per-program setting that controls whether identity binding is off/optional/required (or at minimum, required vs not required).
- When binding is enabled for staff triage:
  - Staff can scan or enter an ID and bind the token to an existing client record, or create a new client record on first-time encounters.
  - Staff can attach multiple IDs to the same client record over time.
  - When no ID is available, staff can bind by name + birth year using a search + select flow, with basic safeguards for ambiguous matches.
- ID numbers are never stored in plaintext, and lookups are implemented in a way that is compatible with our encryption/security guidelines.
- Name search and manual binding flows are restricted to staff-facing triage and are not exposed in public triage flows.
- All binding operations are auditable with who/when/where and method used.
- Existing flows for programs where binding is off behave exactly as they do today (no breaking changes).

Notes
- Priority: **HIGH** – this unlocks both better traceability per person and future attendance/analytics automation, and has direct security/privacy implications.
- Before implementation, confirm the exact config shape and any additional privacy constraints in  and , and update API/UI specs accordingly.]
